name: Source Fidelity review for Mitochondrial Delivery And Transplantation bundle
summary: The staged source, intervention, finding, activity, and outlook records generally stay within the cited PubMed, PMC full-text, and ClinicalTrials.gov source boundaries. The blocking source-fidelity issue is a factual sample-size mismatch in the aged-rat prefrontal-cortex study record: the staged record lists sample_size 12, while the full text reports 24 aged rats across four groups plus 7 young rats used for mitochondrial sourcing.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged study record lists sample_size 12 for the aged-rat prefrontal-cortex stress study.
findings.why_it_matters: The full text reports 24 aged 22-month male rats randomly divided into four experimental groups, plus 7 young 3-month male rats used as the young-mitochondria source. A published study record with the wrong sample size would undermine source fidelity even though the related finding correctly notes n = 3 for plotted biomarker comparisons.
findings.recommended_action: Revise the staged study record to use the correct study-level sample size, or remove sample_size if the curator wants to avoid ambiguity between experimental animals and plotted biomarker replicates.
